summary: A rectangular fortified courtyard with four tall corner towers and terraced inner walls. The build is open-topped with a raised central walkway/roof along one long side, stone foundations and decorative cobble/stone trim. Tower tops have layered flat roofs with dark red central layer and white outer rim; towers show small square windows. Ground inside courtyard is exposed dirt/gravel.

shape.overall_form: rectangular walled enclosure with four corner towers and an internal raised long-sided structure
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (towers at corners, matching trims)

materials.name: stone_slab
materials.role: flat roof edging and walkway slabs

elements: four corner towers with layered flat roofs
rectangular outer wall with stepped/terraced inner face
raised central/side long walkway or roofed corridor
open interior courtyard with dirt floor
rebuild_hints: Place a rectangular outer wall about 18x30 blocks with a single block thick wall and internal stepped terraces on the inner long side.
Erect four tall square towers at each corner (approx 3x3 cross-section) rising ~12 blocks; add 3-4 small square glass windows vertically spaced on the outer faces.
Top towers with stacked flat layered roofs: dark red 5x5 core (nether_brick or similar) surrounded by a 7x7 white slab rim and a single glow block centered.
Add cobblestone/stone blocks at ground level around the perimeter as decorative footing spaced evenly.
Construct a raised long-side walkway/roof along one interior wall using stone slabs with dark red stripe along the center and support posts down to ground.
uncertainties: Exact block types for white rim/edge slabs (assumed stone_slab) and dark red layer (assumed nether_brick) are inferred from color only.
Precise internal layout and height counts are estimated from perspective images; floor count assumed 1 with tall towers.
Footprint dimensions are approximate integers rounded to nearest plausible whole blocks from image scale.
